At a Glance Total Time 45 minutes Number of Questions 35 Percent of Total Score 50% Writing Instrument Pencil required Electronic Device None allowed Instructions Section I of this exam contains 35 multiple-choice questions. For these questions, fill in only the circles for numbers 1 through 35 on your answer sheet. A table of information that may be helpful is in the booklet. Rulers and straightedges may be used in this section. Indicate all of your answers to the multiple-choice questions on the answer sheet. No credit will be given for anything written in this exam booklet, but you may use the booklet for notes or scratch work. After you have decided which of the suggested answers is best, completely fill in the corresponding circle on the answer sheet. Give only one answer to each question. If you change an answer, be sure that the previous mark is erased completely. Here is a sample question and answer. Sample Question and Sample Answer. Chicago is a A. state, B. city, C. country, D. continent, E. village. 5 answer circles are shown, with the correct answer B filled in. Use your time effectively, working as quickly as you can without losing accuracy. Do not spend too much time on any one question. Go on to other questions and come back to the ones you have not answered if you have time. It is not expected that everyone will know the answers to all of the multiple-choice questions. Your total score on the multiple-choice section is based only on the number of questions answered correctly. Points are not deducted for incorrect answers or unanswered questions. The graph is then a straight horizontal line to the right to one point five seconds. It is then a straight vertical line down to about three-fourths of the way below the horizontal axis. The graph is then a straight horizontal line to the right to 2 seconds where it becomes a straight vertical line up to the horizontal axis. The graph moves along the horizontal axis to one second where it becomes a straight vertical line down to about seven-eighths of the way below the horizontal axis. The graph is then a straight horizontal line to the right to one point five seconds. It is then a straight vertical line up to about three-fourths of the way above the horizontal axis. The graph is then a straight horizontal line to the right to 2 seconds where it becomes a straight vertical line down to the horizontal axis. The graph is then a straight horizontal line to the right along the horizontal axis, ending at 2 point 5 seconds. open parenCclose paren shows a graph starting about a fourth of the way above the horizontal axis. The graph is a straight horizontal line to the right to one second.
